basics.name: Junhao Liu
basics.label: PhD Student
basics.summary: A PhD student at Peking University, researching eXplainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI). Dedicated to developing explainability technologies that help humans understand, trust, utilize, and control complex AI systems.

work.name: Tencent
work.position: Research Intern (Project Up), Hunyuan Multimodal Model Team
work.summary: Conducting research and development on HunyuanImage models, focusing on improving model interpretability and controllability, enabling more transparent understanding and precise manipulation of model behavior.

education.institution: Peking University, School of Computer Science

publications.name: Revitalizing Black-Box Interpretability: Actionable Interpretability for LLMs via Proxy Models
publications.summary: A proxy-based explanation framework that transfers explanations from budget-friendly models to expensive LLMs, achieving over 90% fidelity at only 11% of the cost.

publications.name: ReX: A framework for incorporating temporal information in model-agnostic local explanation techniques
publications.summary: A general framework that incorporates temporal information into model-agnostic local explanation techniques such as Anchors, LIME, and Kernel SHAP.

publications.name: Guiding LLM-based Loop Invariant Synthesis via Feedback on Local Reasoning Errors
publications.summary: A framework that provides constructive feedback to LLMs by formally verifying their reasoning process. The tool LORIS achieved 93.1% success rate on 460 C programs.

preprints.name: Focus-LIME: Surgical Interpretation of Long-Context Large Language Models via Proxy-Based Neighborhood Selection
preprints.summary: A coarse-to-fine framework for surgical feature-level interpretation of long-context LLMs via proxy-based neighborhood selection.

preprints.name: ConLUX: Concept-Based Local Unified Explanations
preprints.summary: A general framework that extends local model-agnostic techniques to concept-based explanations supporting attributions, sufficient conditions, and counterfactuals.

preprints.name: WASD: Locating Critical Neurons as Sufficient Conditions for Explaining and Controlling LLM Behavior
preprints.summary: A framework that explains model behavior by identifying sufficient neural conditions for token generation.

preprints.name: MAnchors: Memorization-Based Acceleration of Anchors via Rule Reuse and Transformation
preprints.summary: A memorization-based acceleration framework for Anchors that significantly reduces explanation generation time while maintaining fidelity.

awards.title: Gold Medals, ICPC EC-Final and Asia Regional Contests
Gold Medals, CCPC Finals and Regional Contests
Gold Medal, National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI)

highlights: Introduction to Probabilistic Programming (Graduate Course) - Spring 2024
Data Structures and Algorithms Practice - Fall 2020

projects.name: MTML: A Multi-threaded Language without Data Races and Deadlocks
projects.summary: Designed a multi-threaded programming language based on OCaml, leveraging a type system to statically prevent data races and deadlocks.

projects.name: User-based Collaborative Filtering (Distributed)
projects.summary: Implemented user-based collaborative filtering using Spark and Hadoop, with a comparative study showing Spark's superior efficiency on large-scale workloads.

projects.name: EasyFile: Automated Document Processing Tool
projects.summary: Developed an automated tool for Office and PDF processing, supporting format editing and information extraction.

projects.name: Heuristic EuSolver-based Program Synthesizer
projects.summary: Built a syntax-guided program synthesizer with heuristic rules for CLIA, improving efficiency over standard SMT-based approaches.

projects.name: Java Pointer Analyzer
projects.summary: Implemented a Java pointer analysis tool supporting flow-, context-, and field-sensitive analysis for memory-related bug detection.
